⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘

(If you don't want to read long texts, or you're lazy)

Fifteen hours after the world collapsed into chaos, Kim Dahyun ... a former dance trainer who once dreamed of becoming a idol... finds herself running for her life through a city consumed by the undead. Haunted by years of self-sacrifice and a lifetime of lost dreams, she fights to protect her best friend Hyori, Hyori’s boyfriend Dokja, and a few strangers in a desperate search for safety. But when fate injures her leg and forces her to face death head-on, Dahyun must confront the painful truth she’s always avoided... that sometimes, survival isn’t about fighting harder, but about accepting the end with grace.

⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘⫘

The world had gone to hell in fifteen hours. What used to be just another rainy afternoon had turned into a full-blown nightmare, blood, screams, and the constant sound of something hungry lurking nearby.

Kim Dahyun’s lungs burned as she ran through the parking lot, her black jacket clinging to her sweat-drenched skin. Her long, dark hair whipped behind her, streaked with dirt and rain, and her once-sharp blue eyes now shimmered with exhaustion and fear

Behind her, chaos. A crowd of survivors sprinting for their lives, and behind them.... a horde of zombies pouring out of the shattered glass doors of the mall

“Keep moving! Don’t stop!” Hyori’s voice cut through the noise. She was Dahyun’s high school friend.... still the same hotheaded, fearless girl she used to be. Next to her was Dokja, Hyori’s boyfriend, his face pale but focused as he fired at the swarm chasing them

Someone tripped. A stranger.... Without thinking, Dahyun turned back

“Get up! Come on!” she shouted, grabbing his arm.<
